channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: A fossil site in North Dakota appears to have captured the day the dinosaur-killing asteroid struck Earth, right down to tiny glass beads from the impact lodged in the gills of fish that died within hours - Space Daily
item.description:
1. A fossil site in North Dakota appears to have captured the day the dinosaur-killing asteroid struck Earth, right down to tiny glass beads from the impact lodged in the gills of fish that died within hours  Space Daily
  2. Japan-led team finds layer in Hokkaido showing asteroid impact that wiped out dinosaurs  毎日新聞
  3. New evidence has been found in Japan of an asteroid that may have wiped out the dinosaurs  UA.NEWS
  4. The Chicxulub impact signature in East Asia - Communications Earth & Environment  Nature
  5. What it would have been like to experience the dinosaur‑killing asteroid armageddon: a blow‑by‑blow account  New Pittsburgh Courier

item.title: JWST just mapped the morning weather on a planet 690 light-years away, and the forecast of sand-like clouds exposed a 100-fold bias in how exoplanet atmospheres have been read for more than a decade - Space Daily
item.description:
1. JWST just mapped the morning weather on a planet 690 light-years away, and the forecast of sand-like clouds exposed a 100-fold bias in how exoplanet atmospheres have been read for more than a decade  Space Daily
  2. This exoplanet's sky is full of puffy clouds made of vaporized rock—but only on one side  Scientific American
  3. Webb Telescope Detects Cloudy Mornings And Clear Nights On Alien World  Yahoo
  4. JWST maps the weather on a hot gas giant 700 light-years away  Ars Technica
  5. Astronomers de-fog exoplanet atmospheres with new cloud-detecting method  Phys.org

item.title: A slice of the static on an old untuned television was the afterglow of the Big Bang, which means millions of people spent decades staring at the oldest light in the universe without knowing it. - Space Daily
item.description:
1. A slice of the static on an old untuned television was the afterglow of the Big Bang, which means millions of people spent decades staring at the oldest light in the universe without knowing it.  Space Daily
  2. Cosmically Curious: Before the Big Bang  New Hampshire Public Radio

item.title: Fossil Kept in a Museum For Decades Turns Out to Be a Fearsome New Predator - ScienceAlert
item.description:
1. Fossil Kept in a Museum For Decades Turns Out to Be a Fearsome New Predator  ScienceAlert
  2. ‘T. rex of the sea’: D-FW researchers identify new ancient marine species  Dallas News
  3. Exclusive: There’s a new T. rex in town—and this giant beast ruled the seas  National Geographic
  4. Paleontologists Discover an Ancient Marine Reptile They've Dubbed the T. Rex of the Sea, Crowning Another King of the Cretaceous  Smithsonian Magazine
  5. North Texas fossils reveal giant predator dubbed ‘T. rex of the sea'  NBC 5 Dallas-Fort Worth

item.title: Research Paper Warns That There’s a Massive Experiment at Work to Geoengineer the Earth’s Climate - Yahoo
item.description:
1. Research Paper Warns That There’s a Massive Experiment at Work to Geoengineer the Earth’s Climate  Yahoo
  2. Space launch and reentry environmental concerns are real, but can be mitigated  The Space Review
  3. From Orbital Debris to Atmospheric Pollution: The Hidden Cost of Space Flight  Hampshire Chronicle
  4. Soot from rocket launches remains in the atmosphere for years and has a climate impact 540 times greater than pollution emitted near the Earth's surface. Mega constellations like Starlink will account for 42% of the space sector's climate impact by 2029.  CPG Click Petróleo e Gás
  5. Pollution from rockets like SpaceX's Falcon 9 is bad for atmosphere—but it's also cooling Earth  starlust.org

  3. Earth’s molten core flow 1999 and 2016  European Space Agency
  4. New analysis sheds light on unexpected reversal in Earth’s molten outer core beneath the Pacific  The Watchers - Watching the world evolve and transform

item.description:

  4. Astronomers believe Neptunian moon is lone intact survivor of ancient collision  CNN

item.title: The human genome contains traces of ancient viruses that infected our ancestors millions of years ago — and some of those viral leftovers were later repurposed into genes that help make human pregnancy possible - Space Daily
item.description: The human genome contains traces of ancient viruses that infected our ancestors millions of years ago — and some of those viral leftovers were later repurposed into genes that help make human pregnancy possible  Space Daily

item.title: See A ‘Planet Parade’ As Three Worlds Shine After Sunset This Weekend - Forbes
item.description:
1. See A ‘Planet Parade’ As Three Worlds Shine After Sunset This Weekend  Forbes
  2. The moon shines with a swarm of stars tonight as Jupiter, Venus and Mercury line up nearby  Space
  3. Venus, Jupiter, and Mercury are lining up in the western sky after sunset this week — and a Blue Moon is sliding past Antares — in a run of evening sky events that won't be matched again for years  Space Daily
  4. The moon and Venus  The Lewiston Tribune
  5. Venus and Moon glide side-by-side in May  AZPM News

item.description:

  4. Scientists discover why gold doesn’t ‘rust’  Scientific American
  5. Scientists Identify Atomic Trick That Keeps Gold Shiny  Gizmodo

item.title: A Colorado startup just raised $30 million on a quiet bet that astronauts won't actually be the ones building the moon's first permanent base — robots will get there first - Space Daily
item.description:
1. A Colorado startup just raised $30 million on a quiet bet that astronauts won't actually be the ones building the moon's first permanent base — robots will get there first  Space Daily
  2. Lunar Outpost has big plans for the moon. The new Pegasus lunar rover is just the start  Space
  3. Lunar Outpost co-founder Forrest Meyen on building the ‘backbone’ for critical lunar infrastructure  Interesting Engineering
  4. A Colorado startup just raised $30 million to send a second rover to the Moon — and the real bet isn't on exploration, it's on becoming the construction crew that arrives before the astronauts do  Space Daily

item.description:

item.title: Researchers simulated 30 million routes to the Moon and found a hidden detour through L1 that saves fuel and keeps spacecraft talking to Earth the whole way - Space Daily
item.description:
1. Researchers simulated 30 million routes to the Moon and found a hidden detour through L1 that saves fuel and keeps spacecraft talking to Earth the whole way  Space Daily
  2. Scientists find a hidden route to the moon that saves fuel  Space

  4. Scientists discover a shortcut to the moon that NASA missed  geekspin.co
  5. Scientists Map Most Cost-Effective Route to the Moon Out of 24 Million Possibilities  ZME Science

item.title: Scientists thought Jupiter's moon Europa was ejecting water. Now they're not so sure - Space
item.description:
1. Scientists thought Jupiter's moon Europa was ejecting water. Now they're not so sure  Space

  4. Findings reconsider the existence of Europa's vapor plumes  Phys.org
  5. The case for water plumes on Europa just got much harder to make  AOL.com

item.title: The Apollo astronauts left mirrors on the Moon that scientists still bounce lasers off 57 years later, and the round-trip measurement is precise enough to track the Moon drifting away from Earth at the speed your fingernails grow - Space Daily
item.description: The Apollo astronauts left mirrors on the Moon that scientists still bounce lasers off 57 years later, and the round-trip measurement is precise enough to track the Moon drifting away from Earth at the speed your fingernails grow  Space Daily

item.title: An ancient solar storm left clues in tree rings and a famous poet's diary: 'Red lights in the northern sky' - Space
item.description:
1. An ancient solar storm left clues in tree rings and a famous poet's diary: 'Red lights in the northern sky'  Space
  2. Deadly “red sky” solar storm from 800 years ago discovered in ancient trees  ScienceDaily
  3. A red sky frightened medieval Japan, and buried trees now reveal the solar storm behind it  Earth.com

item.title: The Voyager Golden Record carries a small sample of uranium on its cover, placed there so that whoever finds it can measure the decay and work out how long it has been drifting — a built-in clock for a message engineered to last around a billion years. - Space Daily
item.description:
1. The Voyager Golden Record carries a small sample of uranium on its cover, placed there so that whoever finds it can measure the decay and work out how long it has been drifting — a built-in clock for a message engineered to last around a billion years.  Space Daily
  2. The Voyager probes carry golden records etched with greetings in 55 languages, whale songs, and a woman's brainwaves recorded while she thought about being in love, and the records are designed to remain playable for one billion years drifting through i  Space Daily

item.title: A Strange Black Hole Mystery Has Stumped Physicists Since 1993. Researchers May Finally Have the Answer - Gizmodo
item.description:
1. A Strange Black Hole Mystery Has Stumped Physicists Since 1993. Researchers May Finally Have the Answer  Gizmodo
  2. Crystals of space and time: A structural phenomenon that may collapse into tiny black holes  Phys.org
  3. Tiny black holes may form out of a crystal-like state in spacetime  The Brighter Side of News
  4. The Mathematics That Turns Spacetime Into a Crystal, Then a Black Hole  ScienceBlog.com
